name: KB Full Body Shred
description: A complete full-body kettlebell workout covering all movement patterns — squat, hinge, push, pull, unilateral, and core. Like the Reddit Recommended Routine but all kettlebell. Heavier on lower body than most KB workouts: Bulgarian splits, RDLs, and goblet squats balance out the usual swing-heavy programming. Single bell, ~40 min.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Halo one direction, lunge, switch direction, lunge other leg. Controlled.
Figure-8 pass between legs, then step back into lunge. Alternate.
Full depth, chest up, elbows inside knees at the bottom
Hold KB goblet style at chest. Rear foot on bench/chair/couch. Control the descent.
Slow on the way down, squeeze glutes at the top. Feel the hamstrings.
Explosive hips. This is the power complement to the slow RDL.
Clean to rack, strict press overhead. Alternate arms each set or do all one side then switch.
Hinge position, pull elbow past ribs, squeeze at top
Slow and controlled. Eyes on the bell. This is core stability + hip mobility.
Last push — full power, empty the tank

tips: One kettlebell, ~40 minutes — covers every movement pattern
The Bulgarian splits and RDLs give this way more lower body work than typical KB circuits
Use a moderate bell you can press for 8+ reps — lower body exercises will feel easy but that's fine
For the Bulgarian split squat, any elevated surface works: bench, chair, couch, stairs
Progress by adding reps, then adding a round, then going heavier
Can be done 2-3x per week as a primary training session
